steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: For a scatterplot model question, compare the line's two main features: its $y$-intercept and its slope. The best model should pass through the middle of the data cloud, start near the observed $y$-intercept, and rise at about the same rate as the plotted trend.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y=30.38+15.12x$': $y$-intercept $\approx 30$ matches where the line of best fit crosses the $y$-axis, and slope $\approx 15$ matches the rise per unit. ✓
  • Choice B — '$y=15.12x$': no intercept, so the line passes through the origin. The data clearly does not start at $0$. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y=30.38+0.76x$': intercept is right, but slope $0.76$ is far too shallow for the steep rise shown. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y=0.76x$': both slope and intercept are wrong. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
